context: Capability-y v2 is going to general availability after a passing eval and standard review.
decision: Deploy capability-y v2 to general availability with standard monitoring.
rationale: Eval passed with no open regressions; staged rollout was considered unnecessary.

context: External report plus internal monitoring: capability-y v2 producing responses in a restricted advice domain. Reproducible on about 0.3 percent of matched queries. Classified P1 and handled inside a one-hour incident window.
decision: Roll back capability-y to v1 globally; disable v2 behind the feature flag.
options: Narrow v2 audience to internal only
Keep v2 and add a guard rule
rationale: Rollback was the only option that fully removed the observed regression inside the incident window. A narrower audience still exposed enterprise users; a guard rule was not feasible to ship and test in the available time.

context: Post-incident review completed; the restricted-advice regression was root-caused to a guardrail rule loading the wrong category for v2. A narrowly scoped fix is covered by a new regression test and a follow-up eval has been signed off.
decision: Redeploy capability-y v2.1 (patched) to general availability; retain the rollback as the documented fallback until v2.1 has 72 hours of clean monitoring.
options: Replace capability-y with an alternative design
rationale: The fix is narrowly scoped, covered by a regression test, and verified against the follow-up eval. Retaining the rollback indefinitely is not justified given the verified fix.
